remarks.remark: Introduction lacks a clear thesis statement. UPSC expects precise framing of the central argument in the opening paragraph to anchor the entire answer.
Valid observation but entirely unsupported. Back every claim with constitutional provisions, landmark judgments, or official committee recommendations.
Argument is one-dimensional. Include counter-perspectives and critical comparative analysis to achieve a balanced, high-scoring answer.
Logical inconsistency detected. This claim directly contradicts the argument established in the opening paragraph. Ensure internal consistency throughout to avoid mark deductions.
Fundamental conceptual error. Framework applied here misinterprets the core mechanism entirely. Demonstrates a knowledge gap that requires thorough revision of standard references.
Partially relevant but lacks depth. Expand with socio-economic dimensions and support with quantitative data from government reports.
Critical factual error in this comparative analysis. Such inaccuracies are heavily penalised in UPSC evaluation. Verify all data against official government sources.
Conclusion is generic and fails to synthesise the main arguments. Rewrite to directly address all dimensions of the question with a concrete forward-looking recommendation for maximum marks.
